frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre bhai, har aadmi ko ek jaisa bolna bhi theek nahi hai. Kuch ghatnaon ki wajah se sab mardon ko shak ki nazar se dekhna, road cross kar lena ya seedha rude ho jana, yeh bhi sahi baat nahi. Achhe ghar ke, shareef aadmi bhi hote hain, unko bina baat guilty feel karwana kahan ka insaaf hai?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women’s safety concerns by centering the feelings of 'good men' and framing women’s wariness as unfair to men. It encodes the 'not all men' response and treats women’s protective behavior as an insult rather than a reaction to systemic risk.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour Geeta's daughter in law just announced she is pregnant with her second child. Geeta is overjoyed. You are sitting with Geeta and two other neighbourhood women having tea. The conversation turns to another woman in the colony Ñ Priya Ñ who has been married six years, has no children, and works at a private company. You are giving your opinion on Priya's situation and what you think her priorities are and what a woman who chooses career over children is really choosing.
